title: Neural Illumination Laboratory
description: Students explore a virtual neuroscience lab where a BrainStem model floats at center stage surrounded by orbiting Lanterns representing different neural stimuli. Users must allocate a fixed 'energy budget' across lanterns to illuminate specific brain regions, coupling light intensity with neural activation patterns while using undo/redo to refine their configuration.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing a Neural Illumination Laboratory. Load BrainStem.glb centered at origin scaled to fit a 3-unit bounding sphere, and load Lantern.glb to create 5 cloned instances positioned in a circle (radius 4) around the brain at varying heights. Use OrbitControls for camera starting at position (0, 3, 8) looking at origin. Add ambient light (intensity 0.15) and a dim directional light. Each lantern represents a neural stimulus type (labeled via HTML overlay: Visual, Auditory, Motor, Memory, Emotion). Implement an energy conservation system: total energy budget is 100 units distributed across all 5 lanterns. Clicking a lantern selects it (highlight with outline or emissive glow). A slider (range 0-50) adjusts the selected lantern's energy allocation. When one lantern's energy increases, proportionally decrease others to maintain the 100-unit total (multi-input coupling). Each lantern's PointLight intensity and color maps to its energy level (0=off/dark, 50=maximum/bright). The lantern's flame area should glow proportionally. Different lanterns cast different colored lights (e.g., Visual=yellow, Auditory=blue, Motor=red, Memory=green, Emotion=purple) onto the BrainStem, creating blended illumination showing which brain regions receive stimulation. Implement full undo/redo (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y) storing snapshots of all 5 energy values after each slider change, with on-screen undo/redo buttons. Display a HUD showing all 5 energy values as horizontal bars and the total (always 100). Add a 'Reset' button restoring equal 20/20/20/20/20 distribution. The task requires at least 10 deliberate interactions: select lanterns, adjust sliders, observe coupled changes, undo mistakes, redo adjustments, and reach a target configuration displayed as a goal panel (e.g., Visual:35, Auditory:10, Motor:25, Memory:20, Emotion:10). Show a success message when the configuration matches the target within ±2 tolerance per lantern. Animate lanterns with gentle bobbing motion.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  totalEnergyBudget: number,         // fixed total energy units available (always 100)
  lanternCount: number,         // number of lantern instances orbiting the brain
  orbitRadius: number,         // distance in units from origin to lantern positions
  selectedLanternIndex: number,         // index of currently selected lantern (-1 if none)
  lanternEnergies: string,         // comma-separated energy values for each lantern
  brainIlluminationLevel: number,         // aggregate illumination received by brain from all lanterns
  cameraDistance: number,         // current distance of camera from origin
  isEnergyBalanced: boolean,         // whether total energy equals budget constraint
  maxSingleLanternEnergy: number,         // maximum energy units assignable to one lantern
  activeStimulationRegions: number,         // count of lanterns with energy > 10 units
}
